rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM DRILLING COMPANY, INC. VS. SWINERTON WEBCOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 3, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT THE HERRICK CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is continued to October 24, 2024. The Court did not receive courtesy copies of the Supplemental Declaration of David Weaver. Courtesy copies to be delivered to department 501 no later than October 11, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TOY VS. PETER SPALDING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 3, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANTS LILAH LOHMANN AND JASON QUERTERMOUS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on October 3, 2024 plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to citation to any authorities supporting Plaintiff's argument (Opposition 9:4-11:27) that the three day notice served on a deceased tenant terminated the tenancy and properly forms the basis for this unlawful detainer satisfying all the requirements of a UD action including that (1) a decedent may be properly served with a notice; (2) a decedent may be in possession of the property via subtenants or otherwise; (3) a decedent has a meaningful opportunity to cure the notice to pay or quit, which the decedent failed to do; etc. The Court takes judicial notice of the Death Certificate indicating that Peter Slapping died on June 19, 2023, which is two months before he was served with a 3 Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit; 11 months before the operative complaint was sub-served on he decedent; and 12 months before Plaintiff requested the default of the decedent. If the supplemental brief includes anything other than citations to cases (factually similar to the facts at bar and addressing the situation involving notice to cure or quit served on a deceased tenant; a UD action filed against a deceased tenant; default obtained against a deceased tenant; and eventual judgment based on termination of deceased tenant's tenancy based on notice to cure or quit) or a statement "none found," all the extra language would not be considered. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FROYLAN VALENCIA VS. LINDSAY BRENNAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 3, 2024 line 9. DEFENDANTS LINDSAY BRENNAN AKA LINDSAY SINK AND CHRIS TAVELLI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective as it fails to comply with Regulation 12.14(b)(9). The body of the Notice includes incomplete warnings, which Plaintiff does not dispute in opposition. Attached to the Notice are copies of the SFRO 37.9(i) and (j). Plaintiff does not cite any authority for the proposition that the "warning" required by the Regulation maybe satisfied by providing a complete copy of the Rent Ordinance section. The Court notes that when the Rent Board contemplates an attachment of the SFRO provisions to satisfy a requirement, the Rent Board clearly states so, e.g. Regulation 12.14(b)(6) ("(6) the contents of Ordinance Section 37.9B, by providing a copy of same;") =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
